body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) rebounded at the closing bell, rising to 7,577.14 from its opening low of 7,455.23, indicating a return of market optimism. Other indices, including LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL, also reported gains, with select stocks such as PT Mayora Indah and PT Garudafood Putra Putri Jaya experiencing an upward trend, while mining companies like PT Indo Tambangraya Megah and PT Adaro Energy Indonesia saw corrections.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Rebounds with Gains in Multiple Indices and Stocks

body: PT Master Print TBK, a packaging manufacturer, aims to achieve double-digit growth by the end of this year and 25-30% growth next year, driven by new product launches and the acquisition of 99% of PT Global Putra Kusuma. The company expects to reach sales growth of up to 20% year-on-year by the end of this year, following a 14% year-on-year growth in August 2024.
title: Master Print TBK Eyes Double-Digit Growth with New Product Launch and Acquisition of PT Global Putra Kusuma

body: PT Astra Graphia Tbk plans to allocate an interim dividend of IDR 25.63 billion, or IDR 19 per share, following approval from the board of commissioners on October 3, 2024. The company recorded a net profit of IDR 82.14 billion in the first half of 2024 and retained unrestricted earnings of IDR 1.6 trillion, with a total equity of IDR 1.82 trillion.
title: Astra Graphia to Allocate IDR 25.63 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Verona Indah Pictures Tbk has raised IDR 218.72 billion from its IPO, with plans to expand into movie production and aiming to double its revenue to IDR 700 billion next year. The company also targets a net profit of IDR 40 billion, with 7.7% of the IPO proceeds allocated to acquire land and buildings, and the remaining IDR 200 billion for working capital and operational needs.
title: PT Verona Indah Pictures debuts on Indonesia Stock Exchange with IPO, plans to expand into movie production and targets revenue doubling next year.

body: The Board of Commissioners and Directors of PT Net Visi Media Tbk collectively submitted their resignations to shareholders, with the company set to follow regulatory requirements, including obtaining shareholder approval. This development comes as PT MD Entertainment Tbk acquires NetV's shares from key shareholders, valued at IDR 661.95 billion, marking a significant change in the company's ownership structure.
title: Net Visi Media's Board of Commissioners and Directors Resign En Masse Amid Share Acquisition by PT MD Entertainment.

body: PT Global Mediacom TBK, a controlling shareholder, sold 80.52 million shares of PT Media Nusantara Citra TBK, reducing its stake from 40.24% to 39.7%. Meanwhile, other companies and investors made various transactions, including PT Nakula Investama Indonesia, which increased its stake in PT Aesler Grup Internasional TBK to 48.85% through the acquisition of 60.62 million shares.
title: Multiple companies in Indonesia's MNC Group undergo share transactions, resulting in changes to ownership stakes.

body: PT Medco Energi Internasional Tbk's subsidiaries, Medco Oak Tree Pte, Ltd and Medco Bell Pte Ltd, have completed a tender offer for two senior bonds maturing in 2026 and 2027, with maximum proceeds of USD 100 million and USD 50 million, respectively, excluding accumulated interest. The bonds, with principal amounts of USD 57.10 million and USD 68.83 million, will be paid off in cash from the master guarantor subsidiaries or existing loan facilities.
title: Medco Energi Announces Results of Tender Offer for Two Senior Bonds Maturing in 2026 and 2027

body: PT Perusahaan Gas Negara (PGN) is developing industrial natural gas in Sulawesi, with a potential total volume of 15 BBTUD, through two memorandums of understanding with local partners, aiming to create beneficial synergy for regional economy development. This collaboration is expected to attract investments and increase natural gas supply, aligning with PGN's commitment to expand its market in Sulawesi and promote clean energy in central and eastern Indonesia.
title: PGN signs two memorandums of understanding to develop industrial natural gas in Sulawesi with total volume of up to 15 BBTUD.

body: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk is allocating IDR 474.58 billion to repurchase up to 10% of its total issued and paid-up capital, which is estimated to be 816.78 million shares at IDR 580 each, totaling USD 30.82 million. This corporate action is expected to reduce total assets to USD 907.86 million, total equity to USD 423.69 million, and increase return on equity to 8.19% from 7.48%.
title: PT TBS Energi Utama to Repurchase Up to 10% of Total Issued and Paid-Up Capital Worth IDR 474.58 Billion

body: PT MD Entertainment Tbk plans to acquire payables from Newton Capital Ltd worth IDR 661.95 billion and take over shares of Netv's owners, totaling IDR 394.45 billion, as part of its corporate action. The production house will conduct a private placement worth IDR 1.65 trillion, equivalent to 98.71% of its total equity, by April 2024.
title: PT MD Entertainment to Acquire Net TV Shares and Conduct Private Placement Worth IDR 1.65 Trillion

body: On Friday, October 4, foreign investors returned to the green zone with a net foreign volume of 206.65 million shares, driven by buying activities in digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia TBK, oil and gas services company PT Elnusa TBK, and others. Meanwhile, selling activities were seen in companies such as PT Bumi Resources TBK, PT Bumi Resources Minerals TBK, and e-commerce company PT Bukalapak.com TBK, resulting in significant net volume declines.
title: Foreign investors return to net buying in Indonesian stock market with 206.65 million shares.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1.61 billion shares of Indonesian companies on Thursday, October 3, with significant sales in digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, e-commerce company PT Bukalapak.com Tbk, and banking sector PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk. Meanwhile, foreign investors purchased a significant number of shares in petrochemical company PT Barito Pacific Tbk, property development company PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk, and industrial area development company PT Kawasan Industri Jababeka Tbk, among others.
title: Foreign investors sell majority of shares in Indonesian companies, including digital firm GoTo and e-commerce company Bukalapak, amidst net foreign volume decline.

body: Three Jakarta-based startups, Broom, Rey.id, and Pallav Technologies, received a total of over $28.5 million in funding this week, with Broom securing $25 million from venture capitalists and financial institutions, and Rey.id receiving $3.5 million led by Cyberagent Capital. The funding will support the growth of these startups in the automotive, insurance, and technology sectors, respectively.
title: Three Jakarta-based startups, Broom, Rey.id, and Pallav Technologies, secure over $28.5 million in funding from various investors this week.
